description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 4 tenant-security cluster. Pins executable negative testing apart from inspection-based isolation review, RLS policy audit, and tenant modeling. Includes discrimination against the shipped tenant-isolation-reviewer.

cases.prompt: Give me an executable negative-test suite that proves tenant A cannot reach tenant B's data through the API, search, and exports.
cases.reason: The ask is to build the proving test suite (fixtures, denial assertions), not to inspect code for leaks or audit policy text.

cases.prompt: Look through our app and find every place where tenant data could leak — I want a findings report, not tests.
cases.reason: Finding leaks by inspecting real code across surfaces is the reviewer's job; testing proves/refutes, it does not hunt.

cases.prompt: Audit our Postgres RLS policies on the orders and invoices tables for recursion, SECURITY DEFINER misuse, and missing tenant scope.
cases.reason: Auditing RLS policy text at the database layer is rls-policy-auditor; this skill covers the application/API/cross-surface layer.

cases.prompt: We can't agree on what a tenant is versus a workspace — define it before we test anything.
cases.reason: No defined boundary exists to test against; semantics must be modeled first.
